MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-15-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROBERT PATRICK STICHT, J.D., LLM, TEL. NO. 1-310-889-1950 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. WAYNE K. SNODGRASS, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 1-415-554-4679 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT VICKI HENNESSY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT - As defendant points out, plaintiff fails to plead which "policies and/or practices" are supposedly preempted by 8 U.S.C. 1373, 1644 or "stand as obstacles" to congressional purposes and objectives. (Cmplt. para. 1, 43, 48.) Specificity is critical in a facial challenge such as this, as plaintiff "must establish" that the particular "policies and/or practices" "inevitably pose a present total and fatal conflict with section 1373." (Sturgeon v. Bratton (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 1407, 1420) (emphasis in original).) The demurrer is SUSTAINED and the motion to strike GRANTED, both WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. 1 Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/ru
